rulings.Case Title: IN RE: 4029-4033 JUDAH LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Releasing Mechanics Lien Pursuant To Civil Code Sections 8480 Et Seq.
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 19, 2025 Line 1. PETITIONER 4029-4033 JUDAH LLC.'s Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Releasing Mechanics Lien Pursuant To Civil Code Sections 8480 Et Seq. Off calendar for department 302. The petition may be re-noticed to be heard in department 501, the Real Property Court. That court handles all law and motion matters in real property cases per Local Rule 8.10. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ESPINOZA BAIL BONDS, INC. VS. AMANZE EMENIKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend And Correcct Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 19, 2025 Line 3. PLAINTIFF ESPINOZA BAIL BONDS, INC.'s Motion To Amend And Correcct Judgment. Judgment creditor Espinoza Bail Bonds, Inc.'s motion to amend and correct judgment is denied and its alternate request to vacate and set aside the default judgment filed June 22, 2023 is granted. Plaintiff cites no statutory or case authority, and the court is not aware of any, that allows a judgment creditor whose attorney used an incorrect name in filing suit and seeking entry of judgment may have the judgment corrected to reflect the true name of the party who claims to be entitled to collect on the judgment. This error cannot fairly be characterized as "clerical error." A person entitled to pursue a claim and obtain judgment in his favor presumably knows his own true name. Becasue no opposition was filed and there is good cause to vacate the June 22, 2023 judgment, the alternate request to vacate that judgment is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JASON BROWN VS. ALWAYS TAN SKIN AND BODY, TOTAL BODY CONCEPT INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Certain Of Plaintiff'S Request For Prodction Of Documents Or Things, Set No. Two From Defendant Cecil Russell; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 19, 2025 Line 10. PLAINTIFF JASON BROWN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Certain Of Plaintiff'S Request For Prodction Of Documents Or Things, Set No. Two From Defendant Cecil Russell; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Jason Brown's motion to compel further responses from defendant Cecil Russell to requests 1-5, 9-12, 14-18 and 21 of his second set of requests for production of documents and for monetary sanctions is granted as to requests 16-18 and monetary sanctions of $60 and denied as to requests 1-5, 9-12, 14-15, and 21. Mr. Russell must provide verified further code-compliant responses to requests 16-18 and produce all responsive documents to those requests and pay $60 to Mr. Brown no later than April 4, 2025. Because Mr. Russell provided a verification for his original responses with his opposition papers to this motion, the portion of the motion seeking a verification is moot, yet the failure to provide the verification earlier is sufficient grounds for awarding $60 in monetary sanctions to Mr. Brown. Mr. Russell's responses to requests 1-5 , 9-12 and 21 that after a diligent search he has already produced all documents in his possession is sufficient compliance with his discovery obligations. Mr. Brown may question Mr. Russell about the scope of his asserted diligent search at Mr. Russell's deposition. Mr. Brown has not shown that there is any discovery relevance to his requests 14-15 seeking documents about particular Paycheck Protection Program loans, which are not mentioned in his complaint. Mr. Brown's requests 16-18 seeking documents relating to him that are maintained in compliance with San Francisco Labor and Employment Code 11.6 and complaints of sexually inappropriate behavior by other employees meet the standard for discovery relevance and are not overbroad nor has Mr. Russell shown that any of those requests would be unduly burdensome to comply with. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK, N.A. VS. WILLIAM M WHITLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 19, 2025 Line 9. DEFENDANT WILLIAM WHITLEY's Motion To Set Aside. Defendant William Whitley's motion to set aside the default and default judgment, both of which were entered on June 4, 2024, is granted. The motion is timely and there is good cause for the relief sought, especially in light of California's strong public policy of deciding cases on their merits. Mr. Whitley has until March 28, 2025 to file a responsive pleading to the complaint. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)
